t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>一百多年</color>？等等，我怎么有点印象……
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Over a hundred years</color>? Hang on, that sounds familiar...

t_ch: 啊，对了！那封公司的入职邀请函！
t_en: Ah, yes! That invite!

t_ch: 原来你师傅不是仙舟本地人啊？
t_en: So your master wasn't actually from Xianzhou?

t_ch: 是啊，不过从我认识他开始，他就一直没离开罗浮，比本地人还本地人。生前他还跟我开玩笑说，要不是因为我，他就要去<color=#dbc291ff>星际和平公司</color>了。
t_en: Yes. But since I got to know him, he's never left the Luofu. He was more local than the locals. He used to joke with me that if it weren't for me, he would've gone and joined <color=#dbc291ff>the IPC</color>.

t_ch: 等等…师傅也许没有骗你，我们在翻手稿的时候，也找到了一封星际和平公司设计师入职邀请函！
t_en: Hang on... He may not have been joking around with you. We've found an IPC Designer Invite!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，邀请函在你那儿吧？快拿出来给铖杰瞧瞧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, do you have the invite? Quickly, let's show Chengjie.

t_ch: 入职邀请函，跟我师傅有关？
t_en: A Designer Invite? For my master?

t_ch: 莱恩•玛斯特隆佐…这、这确实是师傅的名字。这日期，和一百二十年前他收我为徒的日子，应该是同一天！
t_en: Ryan Masteronzo... Th—This indeed was my master's name. And this date, was the very same day <unbreak>120</unbreak> years ago when he took me on as his disciple!

t_ch: 师傅竟然选择了我，留在了工造司，却没有去星际和平公司吗……
t_en: He chose me and stayed at the Artisanship Commission. He gave up the Interastral Peace Corporation...

t_ch: 我俩听得一头雾水的，你能告诉我们你和你师傅之间的故事吗？
t_en: We're both confused here. Could you please tell us the story between you and Master Ryan?

t_ch: 嗯…不过，我不是很擅长讲故事，说起来可能会很长。
t_en: Mmm... But I'm not a natural storyteller. This might take a while.

t_ch: 没有关系，请详细讲讲。
t_en: That's cool. Tell me all the details.

t_ch: 工造司从来不拒绝来自仙舟外的学生。他们充满活力、满脑子想法，也期盼着能从仙舟上学得一技之长…莱恩老师就是这样的化外民。
t_en: The Artisanship Commission has never turned away any students from outside of the Xianzhou. They are full of energy and ideas, and are always looking to learn knowledge and skills from the Xianzhou... And Master Ryan was one of those Outworlders.

t_ch: 据说他本来就是研究材料的技术员。来罗浮求学一段时间后，被授予了匠人的身份，算是司里面给留学生学成的证明。
t_en: I think he was originally a materials researcher. After coming aboard the Luofu to study, he was granted the status of craftsman, which is proof of accreditation for foreign students.

t_ch: 一般来说，这样的短生种留学生在艺成后都会离开，要不回到故乡，要不另谋高就。
t_en: Usually, these short-life species leave after graduation, returning to their homeworld or finding other work.

t_ch: 因为他们很快就会意识到，自己的热情和天赋在长生种上百年的经验面前，显得单薄贫瘠。这是时间的鸿沟，很少有短生种能跨越过去，大多数人只能发出一声叹息。
t_en: Because they soon realize that their enthusiasm and talent pale in comparison to what long-life species have seen. This is a gulf of time that very few short-life species can bridge. Most people can only sigh at this barrier.

t_ch: 再加上，长生种工匠之间有着瞧不起短生种的氛围，言谈举止的傲慢，比「时间的鸿沟」更加尖锐伤人。我想，师傅一开始还是想要离开的。
t_en: Plus, the long-life craftsmen often look down on the short-life outworlders with an arrogant attitude that cuts deeper than the "gulf of time." It's no wonder my master wanted to leave at first.

t_ch: 当时的我也处于走人改行的艰难挣扎中。我修学星槎制造，但实在没什么天分，很多老师傅都劝我不必苦熬，甚至不必留在工造司里。
t_en: At the time, I was also struggling with my dwindling career. I studied starskiff manufacturing but didn't have any knack for it. Lots of masters told me to stop and that there was no point in staying at the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 不知为什么，莱恩老师向工造司申请收我为徒。在我人生最灰暗的时候，是他伸出了手，给了我继续下去的动力。
t_en: I'm not sure why, but Master Ryan decided to take me on as an apprentice. Just as I was falling to the rock bottom, he reached out and gave me the strength to keep going.

t_ch: 两个要离开的人，因为偶然的际遇都留了下来，也许这就是「缘分」吧。
t_en: So, two people who planned on leaving ended up staying. That's what you could call "fate," right?

t_ch: 我只是需要有一个人告诉我「愿意收我为徒」。但是师傅他…他明明可以作为星际和平公司的设计师离开的，他又是为什么留下来呢？
t_en: I just needed one person to tell me, "I'll take you on as an apprentice." But my master, he... He could have left and been a top designer at the IPC, but he stayed behind instead. Why?

t_ch: 因为他想继续留在工造司里。
t_en: Because he wanted to stay at the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 因为他从你身上看到了他自己。
t_en: Because he could see himself in you.

t_ch: 他经历过困难的岁月，看到你孤立无援的样子就像短生种的他在工造司内。
t_en: He went through hard times, and seeing you isolated and alone reminded him of being a short-life species at the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 这种心情和同情是不一样的。在他的心里，帮助了你，就如同帮助了过去的他自己一样。你是他的徒弟，也是他最理解的朋友。
t_en: This feeling is not the same as sympathy. Deep in his heart, helping you is like helping his past version of himself. You were his apprentice, and his best friend.

t_ch: 我也没想到这么多。我只记得我们师徒俩，一人愿意教一人愿意学，回想起来这些年来尽是些快乐的回忆，只是时间实在是太快了。
t_en: I never put this much thought into it. I just remember that my master was willing to teach, and I was willing to learn. Looking back, all those years were filled with happy memories, but time passes so fast.

t_ch: 回过神来，师傅头发已经花白了。在一个平平无奇的午后，他说自己有些累了，想要休息一下，却突然就倒在了设计桌前，再也没能醒来。
t_en: Before I realized what was happening, his hair was already gray. One ordinary afternoon, he said he was a little tired and wanted to rest — but he fell down at his worktable, and never woke up again.

t_ch: 时间对每个人都是不公平的。他的时间停滞了，我却还在庸庸碌碌地浪费着时光……
t_en: Time is unfair to everyone. His time has ended, yet here I am, wasting away my mediocre days...

t_ch: 请长话短说。
t_en: Please cut to the chase.

t_ch: 我的老师莱恩是从其他世界来求学的短生种。
t_en: Master Ryan was a short-life species who came here as an outworlder student.

t_ch: 大部分留学生学成后都会离开。因为短生种的经验始终不如积累多年的长生种。工造司的匠人们还隐然有瞧不上短生种的氛围。
t_en: Most students leave after finishing their studies. It's because short-life species simply cannot rack up the centuries of experience that long-life species can. The artisans at the Artisanship Commission also seem to have a bit of a bias against the short-life species.

t_ch: 那些老师傅们挑选学徒要求可高了。我这人天资平平，投师无门、四处碰壁。在我心生退意的时候，没想到是莱恩师傅收留了我。
t_en: The old masters had high requirements for selecting apprentices. I was but a person of mediocre talent, and kept hitting setbacks when looking for tutelage. Just when I was thinking about quitting, I never expected Master Ryan to take me on.

t_ch: 再后来，我们俩师傅和徒弟一个教一个学，彼此都很快乐…但是短生种的师傅很快迎来了自己的极限。
t_en: Later on, we studied and learned together, passing the days happily... But as a short-life species, my master soon reached the limit of his life.

t_ch: 是从什么时候开始的呢？一转眼，他的头发全白了；再一眨眼，师傅突然就倒在了设计桌前。
t_en: When did it all begin? In the blink of an eye, his hair went white. And with another blink of the eye, he'd suddenly fallen over at his desk.

t_ch: 长生种和短生种交往，大多是这样的结局了。
t_en: That's how things usually end when a long-life species makes friends with a short-life species.

t_ch: 我欠师傅的实在是太多了，我这人天性懒惰，做事情一拖再拖。要是我能早一点儿出师，现在也不会在这里空留遗憾。
t_en: I owe my master so much. I'm lazy by nature and always put things off. If I could've graduated earlier, I wouldn't be left here with my regrets like I am now.

t_ch: 现在出师也不算迟。
t_en: It's never too late to graduate.

t_ch: 那你就更不应该放弃了！
t_en: Then you definitely shouldn't be giving up!

t_ch: …遗憾也是一种美。
t_en: ...Regret also has its own beauty.

t_ch: 你先别遗憾美了，现在出师也来得及嘛。
t_en: Don't regret just yet. It's never too late for you to graduate now.

t_ch: 你就把这份「损坏的手稿」当作是师傅留给你最后的考卷，如果你能完成你师傅的星槎设计，那才证明你真的出师了。
t_en: Take this damaged manuscript as the final exam paper left behind by your master. If you can complete your master's starskiff design, then that means you can graduate.

t_ch: 完成「损坏的手稿」，我真的做得到吗？
t_en: Complete the damaged manuscript? Can I really do it?

t_ch: 这就对了！有什么需要我们帮助的就告诉我们一声，如果现在没事儿了……
t_en: That's right! If there's anything we can do to help, just say the word, but if everything's all good now, then...

t_ch: 有的！我想看看星槎制造的整个过程，你们能带我去吗？
t_en: Okay! I want to see the whole manufacturing chain for starskiffs. Can you take me to see it?

t_ch: 这…{NICKNAME}，你看？
t_en: Hmm... {NICKNAME}, how about it?

t_ch: 我也好奇星槎是怎么制造的。
t_en: I'm curious too about how starskiffs are built.

t_ch: 我虽然清楚星槎制造的原理，但从来还没走近瞧瞧那些生产线和生产过程呢。
t_en: I get the basic principles of starskiff manufacturing, but I've never personally been there to see the production line or the process with my own eyes.

t_ch: 帮人就要帮到底，对吧？
t_en: If you're gonna help someone, you gotta see it through, right?

t_ch: 我看你们两个意气相投，而且迴星港我也不熟，要不…我就先回去了？
t_en: Seems like you two get along well, and I'm not familiar with Stargazer Navalia, so how about I head back first?

t_ch: 不行，你也得一起去。
t_en: No, you have to come with us.

t_ch: 打工人何必为难打工人呢……
t_en: That seems to be above my pay grade.

t_ch: 哎，走吧。
t_en: *sigh* Fine, I'll come along.

t_ch: 你看铖杰难得打起精神…
t_en: It's so rare to have Chengjie in good spirits...

t_ch: 要是能让铖杰打起精神的话，我还是去吧……
t_en: If I can cheer up Chengjie, then I'd better go...

t_ch: 你一个匠人，没参与过制造吗？
t_en: You're a craftsman, but you haven't seen how it's manufactured?

t_ch: 我知道的只是教材上关于星槎制造的知识，但从来还没走近瞧瞧那些生产线和生产过程呢。
t_en: All I know are the basic principles of starskiff manufacturing, but I've never personally been there to see the production line or the process with my own eyes.

t_ch: 要不怎么说，我是个不争气的学生啊……
t_en: Why else would I say I'm a failure as a student...

t_ch: （交出邀请函）
t_en: (Hand over the invite)

t_ch: 在你出现之前，留在工造司是一条孤独又难以为继的道路；有了你之后，他就能继续坚持下去了。你是他的徒弟，也是他漫漫长路上的朋友。
t_en: Before you showed up, staying at the Artisanship Commission was a lonely and unsustainable path. But with you, he could keep on with his work. You were his apprentice and his friend along that long road.

t_ch: 因为他想研究星槎。
t_en: Because he wanted to study starskiffs.

t_ch: 你也说了，莱恩师傅本来是研究材料学的。但听你谈起他，说的都是他对星槎设计的热爱。想必他留下，也是因为对星槎的兴趣。
t_en: You even said it yourself. Master Ryan originally researched material science. But you've been talking about him and his passion for starskiff design. Presumably, he stayed because of his love for starskiffs.

t_ch: 短生种的匠人想在工造司里坚持下去，也是需要伙伴和朋友的。你这个修学星槎制造的徒弟，想必能理解他的梦想吧……
t_en: Short-life craftsmen need partners and friends if they're to survive in the Artisanship Commission. As an apprentice of starskiff manufacturing, you must be able to understand his dream...

t_ch: 不对，这是我能为师傅做到的最后一件事情了。我必须做到！我一定要做到！
t_en: No, this is the last possible thing I can do for my master. I have to do it! I'm definitely gonna do this!

t_ch: 要完成师傅的遗作，我得切切实实，像个真正的匠人去体会作品的生产。
t_en: To complete his legacy, I have to be down-to-earth and experience production just like a real craftsman does.

t_ch: 我猜，我也逃不开带路的差事的对吧，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: I guess... I can't escape the work of being a guide, huh, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 唉，对咱们这样没什么天分的人，学业艰辛，我是深有体会啊。{NICKNAME}和我会想办法帮你的。
t_en: Alas, I have a profound understanding of how hard it is for untalented students such as ourselves to study. {NICKNAME} and I will come up with a way to support you.

t_ch: 太好了，事不宜迟，我们这就出发去迴星港！
t_en: That's great. This cannot wait, so let's get going to Stargazer Navalia!

t_ch: 让迴星港的流水线上流转制造自己的设计，这是所有行舟坊工匠的梦想。
t_en: Getting your own designs onto the assembly lines in Stargazer Navalia is the dream of all craftsmen in Xianzhou.

t_ch: 师傅曾经无数次提到自己参与改良的引擎也在这里量产，走进了千家万户。不过，他那引擎，在这一百年间，早就已经被淘汰，变成历史的旧款了。
t_en: Master Ryan mentioned countless times how he helped optimize an engine here that's been mass-produced here and sold all over the place. However, that engine became obsolete over the past century and has fallen to the annals of history.

t_ch: 「好的设计应该是历久弥新的」，明明这也是他老人家经常说的话……
t_en: But he always used to say "good design should be timeless"...

t_ch: …铖杰你就直说吧，要我们怎么帮你？
t_en: ...Chengjie, just tell me straight. How can we help you?

t_ch: 一来这迴星港里还有妖物滋扰。我需要你们保护我；再有就是，我想要迴星港里的视觉资料来辅助手稿。
t_en: First up, there're monsters causing trouble around Stargazer Navalia, so I need your protection. Next, I need some visual data in Stargazer Navalia to complement this manuscript.

t_ch: 换而言之，就是<color=#dbc291ff>绘影图形</color>对吧？
t_en: In other words, some <color=#dbc291ff>visiographs</color>, right?

t_ch: 星槎制造有很多环节，每一环都至关重要。
t_en: There are many steps in the starskiff manufacturing process, and each one is important.

t_ch: 铖杰开始有干劲了吗？
t_en: Has Chengjie started to get motivated yet?

t_ch: 我想重新走一遍星槎制造的历程，我有预感在这里，我能重拾起「造物」的动力。
t_en: I want to go through the process of starskiff manufacturing again. I have a hunch that I can recreate the motivation to "create" once more.

t_ch: 我们不会只是负责拍照而已吧？
t_en: We're not just in charge of taking photos, are we?

t_ch: 我会给你们外行人讲解一下星槎的制造原理，你们也算是不虚此行了吧。
t_en: I'll explain the manufacturing principles to you laypeople, so at least the whole trip won't be in vain.

t_ch: 那我们就赶紧出发吧。
t_en: Then let's get going.

t_ch: 这个巨大的水箱里放着的是…种子？
t_en: This giant water tank holds... seeds?

t_ch: 这并不是水箱，而是星槎培养舱。培养舱里注满了培养液，能够辅助「星槎种子」完成萌芽阶段，确保生物材料能够在营养且可控的环境中成长。
t_en: It's not a water tank, but a starskiff incubator. It's filled with a culture medium that supports the starskiff seed during the germination phase, and it can ensure the biological material grows in a nutritious and controllable environment.

t_ch: 我还是第一次知道，{NICKNAME}你有什么想问的吗？
t_en: This is the first time I've ever heard about this. {NICKNAME}, you got any questions there?

t_ch: 「星槎种子」？
t_en: Starskiff seeds?

t_ch: 没错，顾名思义，它就是「能够长成星槎的种子」。不过它并非是什么植物上长出来的「种子」，而是融合了可塑金属、生物材料、玉兆等等的构造体。
t_en: Yup, as the name suggests, it's a seed that can grow into a starskiff. But it's not a seed that grows into a plant-like structure. It incorporates moldable metals, biological materials, jade abaci, and more.

t_ch: 星槎的全部生长信息都储存在种子之中。也就说，这些种子都是出身自带「设计稿」的，种子会按照着设计规划一步步成长。
t_en: All of the growth data for a starskiff is stored inside its seed. In other words, these seeds come with their own "design manuscripts," and each seed grows step-by-step based on the design plan.

t_ch: 在没有外界干扰、营养充分的情况下，「星槎种子」就会生长出一次成型的龙骨。
t_en: In the absence of any external stimulus and with sufficient nutrition, a starskiff seed will grow into a keel in one go.

t_ch: 培养液？
t_en: Culture medium?

t_ch: 通常来说、其中培养液中的催生化合物比例是较为固定的，但也有根据船只设计进行调整的时候，根据成分比例不同，船身的强度、成型速度也是不同的。
t_en: Generally speaking, the ratio of catalyzers in the culture medium is fixed. However, it can be adjusted based on the design of the ship. Depending on the composition ratio, the strength and growth speed of the hull will vary.

t_ch: 所以也有工造司的工匠专门负责研究培养液的配比，为的就是在萌芽阶段将船只打造得更轻盈、更耐用、也更安全。
t_en: So there are craftsmen from the Artisanship Commission who specialize in researching the ratio needed for the culture medium during the germination phase, so as to make the ship lighter, more durable, and safer.

t_ch: 在制造星槎的流水线上有着许多形似圆拱门的「培养液注射器」，为的就是在星槎培育的不同阶段，对星槎成型有对应的微调。
t_en: There are many cylindrical "culture medium injectors" at various phases along the starskiff assembly line to make fine-tuned adjustments to it as it grows.

t_ch: 萌芽阶段？
t_en: Germination phase?

t_ch: 星槎培育大体上可以分为四个阶段——萌芽阶段、雏形阶段、育成阶段、以及成型阶段。你现在看到的就是萌芽阶段。
t_en: There are four main stages in the growth of a starskiff: The germination phase, the embryonic phase, the nutritive phase, and the forming phase. What you're seeing right now is the germination phase.

t_ch: 在这一阶段里，「星槎种子」内部的「息壤」和「木无患」等材料会以玉兆上的信息为基础，长出最初的「胚芽」、「胚轴」的结构。
t_en: During this phase, materials such as fallow earth and Ligneous Freeroamers inside the starskiff seed will form the earliest signs of seed germ and embryo.

t_ch: 如果生长过程不太顺利，则需要及时清理出失效的星槎种子，并且销毁金属框架内的生物材料。
t_en: If anything goes wrong during the growth process, we need to clear out the failed starskiff seed and destroy the biological matter in the metal frame.

t_ch: …星槎是由种子长出来的？
t_en: ...Starskiffs grow out of seeds?

t_ch: 我最好奇的也是这个问题。我每天都会乘坐星槎，没想到它是从种子里长出来的。
t_en: That's what made me most curious, too. Every day I'll be catching one of these starskiffs, without knowing that it's actually been grown from a seed.

t_ch: 你们应该听说过「建木」的传说对吧。据说那东西给仙舟上带来了一场技术变革。特别是丹鼎司，他们曾拥有堪称奇迹的技术……
t_en: You must have heard of the legends of the Ambrosial Arbor, right? That's what brought about a technological revolution on Xianzhou. Especially the Alchemy Commission, they had an utterly miraculous technology...

t_ch: 不过「建木」被帝弓斫断之后，大部分技术都失传了。 但星槎种植技术依然延续了下来。
t_en: But after Reignbow severed the Ambrosial Arbor, most of the tech was lost. However, this starskiff growing technology was retained.

t_ch: 帮我给这个培养舱拍个照吧。拍好了，我们就去雏形阶段。
t_en: Help me take a photo of this incubator. Then we can go check out the embryonic phase.

t_ch: 好像，我已经能看到星槎大概的样子了…真的是从种子长成了船啊！
t_en: It's like I can already see the form of a starskiff taking shape... They really do grow out of seeds into a ship!

t_ch: 就算是我在资料上看过千八百次，到现场一看，我还是觉得不可思议。
t_en: Even though I've read about it a thousand times, I still feel a sense of the incredible in the face of such wonder.

t_ch: 在雏形阶段，是星槎塑形最重要的阶段。如果不注意温度、培养液的配置、或者是设计信息有所缺漏，那么星槎种子便不会按照设计意图生长。
t_en: The embryonic phase is the most important stage in shaping the starskiff. If you don't regulate the temperature and culture medium properly, or if the design data has any errors, then the starskiff seed will fail to grow as designed.

t_ch: 也就是说，在这一阶段还是可能有中途报废的可能？
t_en: That is to say, during this phase, there's still a possibility of chucking out a failed seed?

t_ch: 没错，有一些快速生长的坏品星槎甚至能冲破培养舱，导致培养液泄露事故。一般来说，这些培养舱就只能废弃掉了。
t_en: That's right. Some fast-growing anomalies can even burst through the incubator, causing the culture medium to leak out. When that happens, the incubator basically just gets trashed.

t_ch: 废弃的培养舱？
t_en: A trashed incubator?

t_ch: 即便工匠们对自己的设计十分自信，但是总有一些设计失败的时候。
t_en: Despite the confidence in their own designs, even our craftsmen have their failures.

t_ch: 你看那边的培养舱，应该就是没有考虑到「息壤」的自生长的算法…想要完成师傅的设计，我也得注意一下星槎的生长规律。
t_en: Check out that incubator over there. Its designer must've miscalculated the self-growth algorithm of Fallow Earth... If I want to complete these designs, I need to focus on these growth patterns of starskiffs.

t_ch: 之前发生过严重的事故？
t_en: Has any serious accident ever happened before?

t_ch: 我也是听公输师傅说起过，很久以前迴星港被一伙求药使袭击过。
t_en: I heard Master Gongshu talk about how Stargazer Navalia was attacked by some Elixir Seekers before.

t_ch: 据说是他们得到了建木的样本，希望用星槎培养液培育新的建木。但是，后来他们发现自己上～当～了～！那不过是丹鼎司里的一棵盆栽。
t_en: Rumor has it, they got a sample of the Ambrosial Arbor and hoped to use some starskiff culture medium to grow a new Ambrosial Arbor of their own. However, they were actually getting the wool pulled over their eyes! It was actually just a bonsai tree from the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 他们将小树枝放进培养液里，就像是引爆了一颗炸弹，一瞬间他们藏身的小楼被迅速生长的大树撑破坍塌。
t_en: When they dropped their bonsai cutting into the culture medium, it was like detonating a bomb — in a flash, their hideaway had a massive tree burst through the roof, causing the whole building to collapse.

t_ch: 那棵巨树也在下个瞬间变成了枯木死去。那段小树枝，在一瞬间获得旺盛的生命力，也在一瞬间将生命燃烧殆尽，实在是有些可怕。
t_en: That massive tree spent the next few seconds withering before dying off. And that small twig gained incredible vitality within seconds, and then exhausted all life within a few more seconds. It really was a terrifying phenomenon.

t_ch: …看起来种子要被包裹起来了。
t_en: ...Looks like this seed is about to be wrapped up.

t_ch: 嗯，成型的星槎是看不到核心部分的。一方面是在后续阶段，星槎种子会生发出细致且复杂的「根系」，这是星槎内植的玉兆网络。
t_en: Mmm, you can't actually see the core part of a starskiff that has taken form. For one thing, the starskiff seeds develop a detailed and complex root system during subsequent phases, which also acts as the internal jade abacus network aboard each starskiff.

t_ch: 另一方面是在外部最坚固的生物材料会逐渐闭合，星槎在遭遇剧烈冲击的情况下，内部也能维持核心的运作。
t_en: For another thing, the toughest biological materials on the exterior will gradually close up, so that the starskiff can continue its core internal operations even when it encounters severe impacts.

t_ch: …我已经开始听不懂了。
t_en: ...I'm already starting to lose track of everything being said.

t_ch: 帮我给这个培养舱拍个照吧。拍好了，我们就去育成阶段。
t_en: Help me take a photo of this incubator. Then we can go check out the nutritive phase.

t_ch: 哦哦哦！我已经好像看到星槎的模样了，这就是育成阶段吗？
t_en: Oh oh, I think I can see the shape of a starskiff! Is this the nutritive phase?

t_ch: 培养舱难道也会一起生长吗？
t_en: Do the incubators grow alongside the starskiffs themselves?

t_ch: 应该不是，这就是换了一个更大的培养舱而已吧。
t_en: I don't think so. This is just a bigger incubator.

t_ch: 看来就快要培养完成了！
t_en: It looks like it's almost done incubating!

t_ch: 嗯嗯，虽然我什么都没有做，却油然而生一种自豪的感觉了。
t_en: Mmm, even though we haven't done a thing, there's still this spontaneous sense of pride.

t_ch: 你说是吧，铖杰。
t_en: Don't you think so, Chengjie?

t_ch: …铖杰？
t_en: ...Chengjie?

t_ch: 坏了，铖杰走丢了！
t_en: Darn it, Chengjie's missing!

t_ch: 救命——
t_en: Help—

t_ch: 我只是想来这边看看星槎的设计，救命——
t_en: I just came here to admire the starskiff's design, then— Help!

t_ch: 哎呀，我都叮嘱过让他别乱跑了。走吧，救人要紧！
t_en: Oh no, I told him not to run around. Let's go. We gotta save him!

t_ch: 不行啊，要是对一项工作产生自豪感，那就不能毫无愧疚地摸鱼了……
t_en: Oh no, if I take pride in any of the work, then I can't just slack off without feeling guilty...

t_ch: 多谢两位相救，要不是你们，我可能就没法完成师傅的梦想了……
t_en: Thank you for saving me. If it weren't for you, I wouldn't be able to fulfill my master's dream...

t_ch: …你这「师傅的梦想」可真是块膏药，哪里需要往哪里贴。
t_en: Well, it's starting to seem like you are just using your master's dream as an excuse to wallow in self-pity.

t_ch: 啊，抱歉……
t_en: Oh, I'm sorry...

t_ch: 你说的对，我整天说着「师傅的梦想」。但我已经快对「梦想」这个词感到麻木了。
t_en: You're right. I've been talking about my master's dream all day. But I'm quickly growing numb to the concept of this word, "dream."

t_ch: 一开始每次想到莱恩先生的脸，我都会泪流满面，心里就有一股子冲劲。
t_en: At first, I'd burst into tears whenever I thought of Master Ryan's face, and my heart would skip a beat.

t_ch: 现在，记忆里莱恩老师的脸已经模糊不清了。「师傅的梦想」就像是心里面一处好的差不多的伤疤，为了不至于让我忘了疼，我隔一阵就撕挠一下。
t_en: But now, Master Ryan's face is like a blur to me. And my master's dream, it's like a thick old scar on my heart — an itch that I scratch every now and again, so that I'll never forget his memory.

t_ch: 我快要分不清自己到底是想实现老师的梦想，还是只是在提醒自己不要忘记他。
t_en: Now I can't even tell if I really do want to fulfill my master's dream, or whether I'm just reminding myself over and over not to forget about him.

t_ch: 我、我怕有一天想起莱恩时，心里再也没有一丝波动。那对我来说就意味着师傅真正、永远离开了我的生活。我该怎么回报他对我付出的时间呢？
t_en: I—I'm afraid that one day, I'll think of Master Ryan, and there won't be that stirring in my heart anymore. That'll mean to me that my master has truly left my life, forever. How on earth could I ever hope to repay the time he has spent with me?

t_ch: 我要趁现在，就趁着「师傅的梦想」留有余温时，我一定要做到它。
t_en: I gotta do this when this dream of my master has not completely faded away in my mind. I have to finish it for him.

t_ch: 哎呀，我光顾着自言自语了…还是说回培养舱和星槎吧。
t_en: Oops, ha! I've been talking to myself again... Let's go back to the incubator and all those starskiffs, yeah?

t_ch: 你提到了星槎的引擎？
t_en: You mentioned starskiff engines before?

t_ch: 船尾有一块圆形的空缺，那就是用来组装「玦轮」的地方，也就是我刚才提到的星槎引擎。那是一种整合了重力控制与动力的玉兆装置。
t_en: There's a circular cutaway on the tail which is where the jade wheel gets installed — that's the starskiff engine I was just talking about. It's an integrated jade abacus that controls gravity and motion.

t_ch: 迴星港里的穹仪也是运用了相近的技术原理。
t_en: The Meridian Pin at Stargazer Navalia also operates using similar tech principles.

t_ch: 不过，很多非法改造星槎的人会借此钻空子，他们会把军用斗舰的玦轮装在民用星槎上…出事故的人也不在少数，毕竟那是不符合规格的做法，很危险。
t_en: But lots of illegal modders will exploit this, installing military-grade jade wheels onto civilian starskiffs... And many have paid the price with their lives. After all, it's all illegal and highly dangerous.

t_ch: 培养舱难道也会一起生长吗？
t_en: Do the incubators grow alongside the starskiffs themselves?

t_ch: 应该不是，这就是换了一个更大的培养舱而已吧。
t_en: I don't think so. This is just a bigger incubator.

t_ch: 看来星槎就快要完成了。
t_en: Looks like the starskiff is almost ready.

t_ch: 是啊，这个培养舱也帮我拍个照吧。
t_en: Yeah, help me snap a shot of this incubator here.

t_ch: 完全成长的星槎会从培养舱里取出，经过组装之后就暂时停靠在这里。
t_en: Fully-grown starskiffs are extracted from their incubators and temporarily moored here following assembly.

t_ch: 不久之后，它们将驶向仙舟各处，成为你我身边的风景的一部分。
t_en: Before long, they'll sail all over Xianzhou and become a part of the scenery around us.

t_ch: 星槎还真是厉害呢。
t_en: Starskiffs really are a miracle of nature.

t_ch: 星槎还真是厉害呢。
t_en: Starskiffs really are a miracle of nature.

t_ch: …请你原创一下自己的感想。
t_en: ...Can't you please curate your own thoughts.

t_ch: 感觉我也会生产星槎了呢。
t_en: One does not simply sail without a starskiff.

t_ch: 自动生产系统真好啊！为什么太卜司还需要咱们这么辛苦工作呢？
t_en: Yep. And the way they are automatically produced is so awesome! Why does the Divination Commission still need us to work so hard for them?

t_ch: 星槎乘风会有时，直挂云帆济星海。
t_en: To boldly sail where no starskiff has sailed before.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，看不出来你还是个诗人。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I didn't know you were a poet.

t_ch: 铖杰，还要照相吗？
t_en: Any more photos, Chengjie?

t_ch: 就帮我拍一个我和星槎的合照吧，麻烦你了。
t_en: Help me snap a pic together with this starskiff. Thank you.

t_ch: （小声）虽然平仄格律有很大的问题……
t_en: (Whispering)...Wait a minute, why does that line of poem {F#she}{M#he} just said sound so familiar, huh...

t_ch: 和你们一起走在迴星港的路上时，我其实一直很不安。
t_en: I've actually been quite uneasy during the whole of our journey to Stargazer Navalia.

t_ch: 我真的能做出星槎的设计吗？我真的能完成师傅的梦想吗？还是说，我来这里只是想寻找一个新的逃避方式？
t_en: Can I really design starskiffs? Can I really fulfill my master's dream? Or am I just looking for some kind of escape from reality?

t_ch: 你想的也太多了嘛。
t_en: You're overthinking this.

t_ch: 对呀，我见你第一面的时候，就觉得你这个人责任感也太重了，放轻松吧。
t_en: Yeah, when I first met you, I could feel the heaviness of your burden. Please take it easy.

t_ch: 谢谢你青雀小姐，现在我已经不再想这么多了。我只想马上回去，赶紧完成师傅的手稿。
t_en: Thank you, Miss Qingque. I'm not overthinking everything anymore. I just want to go back right away and help complete my master's manuscript.

t_ch: 现在你还在不安吗？
t_en: Are you still uneasy?

t_ch: 我已经没有不安了。
t_en: No, I'm better now.

t_ch: 我现在只想马上回去，赶紧完成师傅的手稿。
t_en: I just want to go back right away and help complete my master's manuscript.

t_ch: 这趟旅行对工匠的帮助不大。
t_en: This trip doesn't seem to be helpful for a craftsman, though.

t_ch: 你也说话太直了……
t_en: Ha, you don't sugarcoat things, do you?

t_ch: 你说的没错，我在来之前也在怀疑自己，我无比熟悉星槎制造的每一步，真的有必要来这里实地看一看吗？
t_en: You're right. I doubted myself before coming here. Since my knowledge about every step of the starskiff manufacturing process is top-notch, was it really necessary to come here to take a look at things for myself?

t_ch: 但是幸好我来到了这里。我重新收获了动力，此时此刻只想赶紧完成师傅的手稿。
t_en: But luckily, I'm here. I've got my motivation back, and now all I can think about is completing my master's manuscript as soon as I can.

t_ch: 我虽然忘记了师傅的模样和性格，但是他教给我的手艺、他教导我时说过的话，早就已经成为了我的小习惯，我的思考方式。
t_en: Despite forgetting my master's appearance and personality, the craftsmanship and words that he passed on to me have long since helped me form my habits and way of thinking.

t_ch: 师傅为了教导我、为了点燃我的梦想，付出了心血和时间…他把「时间」这个对短生种最苛刻的东西全部倾注在了我身上，我必须回报他。
t_en: My master spent so much time and effort on teaching me, on igniting my passions... He gave me all his time, the most precious thing for a short-lived being. So I have to repay him in kind.

t_ch: 从刚才起，我就一直在想，还有什么能够超越时间的桎梏？
t_en: From that moment on, I've been constantly wondering, is there anything that can transcend the confines of time?

t_ch: 杰出的作品！
t_en: A masterpiece!

t_ch: 我也是这样想的。
t_en: That's what I was thinking, too.

t_ch: 伟大的思想！
t_en: A world-changing idea!

t_ch: 铖杰，你有伟大的思想吗？
t_en: Chengjie, have you got any of those world-changing ideas up there?

t_ch: …很遗憾，我没有，我师傅大概也没有。
t_en: ...Unfortunately, I don't. And I don't think my master does, either.

t_ch: 师徒的传承？
t_en: The passing down of knowledge?

t_ch: 有道理，要是铖杰再去收个徒弟，徒弟再去收徒弟…就能超越时间了吧？
t_en: Makes sense. If you take on an apprentice, and then that apprentice takes on their own apprentice... Wouldn't that transcend time?

t_ch: 这也确实是个办法，不过，我还没有达到能收徒弟的水准。
t_en: That's actually a pretty good idea, but I haven't reached the level of being able to take on an apprentice yet.

t_ch: 没有吧。
t_en: Nothing.

t_ch: …你怎么比我还消极。
t_en: ...You're even more of a pessimist than I am.

t_ch: 师傅留下的手稿还没能问世，一旦完成，我相信它一定能成为跨越时间的杰作。
t_en: My master's manuscript has never been published before. Once finished, I trust it will become a masterpiece that transcends the confines of time.

t_ch: 师傅为了教导我、为了点燃我的梦想，付出了心血和时间…他把「时间」这个对短生种最吝啬的宝物倾注在了我身上，现在是我用「时间」回报他了。
t_en: My master spent so much time and effort on teaching me, on igniting my passions... He gave me all his time, the most precious thing for a short-lived being. So I have to repay him in kind.

t_ch: 你们看，这迴星港每天有这么多的星槎出港。总有一天，我也要将我师傅的梦想之船造出来，轻轻将它推出去，让它在星槎海最繁荣的航道里飞行！
t_en: Just look: Every day, there are so many starskiffs leaving Stargazer Navalia. One day, I'll build my master's dreamship, push it out gently, and set it sailing in the busiest lane of Starskiff Haven!

t_ch: 等到那一天，我就是真正地出师了！想必师傅也会认可我的。
t_en: When that day comes, I'll be a real graduate! And my master will approve of me, I'm sure.

t_ch: 加油，去完成它吧！
t_en: Go for it, get it done!

t_ch: 别在这里光说不练。
t_en: Time to walk the walk, then.

t_ch: 你别担心，我现在浑身充满了动力，回去就要大干一场。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm filled with motivation now. Once I get back, I'll go all out.

t_ch: 我还有一个不情之请，我这人太懒惰了，请你们一定要监督我，监督我完成师傅的梦想！
t_en: I still have a presumptuous request to make of you. I'm just so lazy, so can you please supervise me and make sure that I accomplish my master's dream?

t_ch: 要不{NICKNAME}你去监督吧……
t_en: Well, then, how about you keep an eye on him, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 请{NICKNAME}大人一定要监督我完成！
t_en: Please, {NICKNAME} — please supervise me until I'm done!

t_ch: 我会回工造司再去找你的。
t_en: I'll be looking for you back at the Commission soon.

t_ch: 只要不太麻烦的话……
t_en: As long as it's not too much of a hassle...

t_ch: 我感觉现在浑身充满了动力，我要回去大干一场，我先走了。再次感谢二位的帮助！
t_en: I feel like I'm filled with motivation now. Once I get back, I'll go all out. I'm off, then. Thanks again to both of you for all your help!

t_ch: 终于是了却一桩难题，多谢了。我好累啊，我也先回去了，之后再联系吧。
t_en: Finally, that's resolved. Thank you so much. I'm exhausted, so I'll go back now too. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 我要证明他的时间没有白费，他的时间还将我的身上流淌下去，还将在他的作品上流淌下去。
t_en: I want to prove that the time he's spent was not wasted. That his time spent will flow on through me, and that it will flow on through his surviving works.

t_ch: 我就知道太卜大人把我支到工造司来肯定没有什么简单的工作。
t_en: I knew it was gonna be trouble when the master diviner assigned me to the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: …我明明就只是一个看管书库的司库。
t_en: ...Seriously, I'm just a lowly custodian who looks after the repository.

t_ch: 这一路上怎么全是入魔机巧，都交给你了。
t_en: Why are there all these Entranced Ingenia along the way? You take them, here.

t_ch: 你要是处理完之后叫我啊。
t_en: Come find me once you take care of everything.

t_ch: 星槎的萌芽阶段已经拍摄成功了。
t_en: Photos have been taken of the starskiff in germination phase.

t_ch: 星槎的雏形阶段已经拍摄成功了。
t_en: Photos have been taken of the starskiff in embryonic phase.

t_ch: 星槎的育成阶段也已经拍摄成功了。
t_en: Photos have been taken of the starskiff in nutritive phase.

t_ch: 从这个角度看，很多细节都看不到，我们换个地方再拍一个其他角度的吧。
t_en: Many details aren't visible from this angle. Let's move to shoot from a different angle.

t_ch: 这张拍得好，真是麻烦你了。
t_en: This is a great shot. Thank you so much.

t_ch: 你来了啊，太好了！我在这里一边研究设计稿，一边等你。
t_en: You're finally here! I've been studying the design manuscript while waiting for you.

t_ch: 没想到你真的在等我。
t_en: I didn't think you would really wait for me.

t_ch: 你这么说就生分了，经过上一次迴星港一别，我已经将你和青雀小姐看作是我的挚友了！朋友要来，我怎么可能擅自离开嘛。
t_en: I'm disappointed to hear that. Since we parted ways at Stargazer Navalia, I've seen you as my close friend! How can I go away when I'm expecting a friend's visit?

t_ch: 设计稿的修改还顺利吗？
t_en: How's it going with the manuscript?

t_ch: 说实话，一开始我还在担心如今做出一百年前的星槎会不会没有什么实际意义。
t_en: Honestly speaking, I was worried whether it would be meaningless to make a starskiff from a century ago at first.

t_ch: 但现在看来，我的担忧是多余的，莱恩老师的设计稿肯定是能超越时代的，即便是一百年后的现在，它仍然站在设计最前沿，老师真是太厉害了！
t_en: But now, I've realized my concern is unfounded, as Master Ryan's design is undoubtedly timeless. Even though the design is century-old, it still stands at the forefront of the trend. I'm so impressed!

t_ch: 何以见得？
t_en: Care to elaborate?

t_ch: 我根本看不懂！它和现在的星槎设计完全是两种思维方式！
t_en: I can't understand it at all! The concept it uses is totally different from the one adopted by the modern starskiff designs!

t_ch: 看不懂你还要造它？
t_en: You sure you wanna make it when you can't understand it?

t_ch: 师傅比我厉害太多了，我看不懂也很正常，就是得多花些时间。
t_en: My master is way better at this than me, so I'm not too surprised that I can't understand his work. I just need to spend more time studying it.

t_ch: 很担心你能不能完成。
t_en: I'm worried you won't be able to complete it.

t_ch: 谢谢担心，但咱可不能就这么放弃了啊。
t_en: Thanks for your concern, but we can't give up so easily.

t_ch: 不太可能吧…
t_en: I don't think you can make it...

t_ch: 仙舟的星槎当然是无可挑剔，但是设计语言确实有些陈旧了。老师的新设计肯定能给星槎带来一场革命的。
t_en: The starskiff of Xianzhou is great, but the design is rather old-fashioned. I'm sure my master's design will bring about a revolution.

t_ch: 师傅真是太厉害了！
t_en: Your master was a genius!

t_ch: 只有你和我知道莱恩老师这么厉害还不行，我得让所有人都知道。
t_en: It's not enough that only you and I know how awesome Master Ryan is. The whole world should know of it.

t_ch: 所谓实践出真知嘛，在解读手稿的过程中，正是我这个不肖徒儿和师傅对话的过程，也是一步步接近师傅理想的过程。
t_en: Sometimes, you just need to jump in to get your feet wet. While studying the manuscript, I'm interacting with my master in an attempt to get closer to his way of thinking.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，也多亏了你来这里，让我更加有动力了。请你之后也要来监督我，鞭策我！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, your presence really motivates me. Please drop by regularly to monitor my progress and encourage me!

t_ch: 啊，这么读着手稿，感觉就好像老师活了过来，重新站在我背后，指点我的课业。那些原本淡忘的温暖回忆，现在都回来了啊。莱恩老师，请再给我点时间！
t_en: When I'm studying the manuscript, it feels as though my master has come back to life. I can feel him standing behind me, and guiding me in my work. Those heartwarming memories that have long faded away all surge back to my mind. Master Ryan, please give me more time!

t_ch: 有种指导孩子写作业的疲劳感…
t_en: I feel like I'm helping kids with their homework...

t_ch: 等星槎造成的那一天，你会觉得一切是有回报的！为了纪念咱俩的努力，我会在船身上刻下它的名字「莱恩师傅、铖杰与{NICKNAME}号」。
t_en: Your effort will pay off the day the starskiff is made! To commemorate our hard work, I'll engrave the name — "Ryan, Chengjie & {NICKNAME}" — on the ship.

t_ch: 来，这是我答应给你的仙人快乐茶，每天一杯，干活不累。
t_en: Here you go, this is the Immortal's Delight that I promised you. One cup a day, all work is just play.

t_ch: 咱俩背后可一个人也没有啊…
t_en: Um... There's no one standing behind us, right?

t_ch: 只是个比方而已，别紧张。仙舟上没什么怪力乱神的东西。
t_en: Relax. It's just a figure of speech. There's nothing supernatural on Xianzhou.

t_ch: 来，喝口这个压压惊。这是我答应给你的仙人快乐茶，每天一杯，干活不累。
t_en: Here you go, this is the Immortal's Delight that I promised you. One cup a day, all work is just play.

t_ch: 好累，我已经…燃尽了……
t_en: I'm exhausted... I'm totally... drained...

t_ch: 你好，不管你是谁，请别在意，我只是想一个人在这儿躺一会儿。
t_en: Hi, there. No matter who you are, please don't be bothered by my presence. I just want to lie down and be alone for a while.

t_ch: 啊…{NICKNAME}，是你啊。那就麻烦你开始，给我来点刺激，好好教训我一下，让我振作起来！
t_en: Oh... {NICKNAME}, it's you. I'd appreciate it if you could talk some sense into me and help me pull myself together!

t_ch: 你这…坨…扶不上墙的…烂泥？
t_en: You're... a hopeless good-for-nothing?

t_ch: 嘶…我心底毫无波动，甚至还有点想笑？我想想，你该这么骂我……
t_en: Tsk... It does nothing to me. In fact, I even have an urge to laugh! Well, I think you should say something like this...

t_ch: 我就是个毫无天赋的白痴，日复一日混吃等死的蛀虫，宇宙中最没用的就是我脖子上那玩意，空空如也没有一丝灵感，还不如一个黑洞……
t_en: I'm an idiot with no talent and a worthless bug who achieves nothing in his life. That thing on my neck is the most worthless thing in the universe. It's empty inside, without a single idea. It's worse than a black hole...

t_ch: 你这不是挺清楚的吗？
t_en: I think you can do it better than me.

t_ch: 是啊。但是这话从自己嘴里说出来，就像是用自己的手给自己挠痒痒，谁会笑啊？
t_en: Indeed, but when I'm the one who says it, I can't feel the impact of those words.

t_ch: 请不要侮辱白痴、蛀虫和黑洞…
t_en: Please don't insult idiots, bugs, and black holes by associating yourself with them.

t_ch: 开始了，开始有感觉了…心底泛起了一丝生气。等等，唉，它又消失了。
t_en: I'm feeling it... There's a spark of anger rising deep down inside me. Hold on. Alas, that feeling's gone.

t_ch: 羞辱不能带来持久的动力…
t_en: Humiliation can't keep you going for long...

t_ch: 是这个道理，我也知道。靠着喊口号，自我勉励，始终是有极限的，为什么其他工匠师傅能持之以恒地做下去呢？
t_en: I know. There's only so much that self-motivation can do. I wonder how other craftsmen manage to stick with their jobs so persistently.

t_ch: 师傅把他最珍贵的时间都给了你…
t_en: Your master gave you his precious time...

t_ch: 这不是我自己说过的话嘛…对啊，老师明明将那么重要的时间赠予我，我为什么还在这里一动不动呢？
t_en: Isn't that what I've said before? Why am I not doing anything here when my master has given me his precious time?

t_ch: 有没有想过干脆不做工匠了？
t_en: Have you ever thought of giving up?

t_ch: 想过。这两百年来一直都有想过。
t_en: I have. It's been on my mind for the past <unbreak>200</unbreak> years.

t_ch: 但是就像是我说过的，莱恩老师将他最重要的时间给了我。如果我做不成匠人了，那些最珍贵的时光就这样彻底浪费了，不复存在了。
t_en: But as I said, Master Ryan gave me his precious time. If I give up now, that time will be wasted and cease to exist.

t_ch: 我，必须成为工造司的匠人，这样才能让一切有意义。
t_en: I must become a craftsman of the Artisanship Commission to make everything worthwhile.

t_ch: 再努力一下，这是你师傅的梦想。
t_en: Try it again. This is your master's dream.

t_ch: 师傅的梦想……
t_en: My master's dream...

t_ch: 是啊，莱恩老师将他最重要的时间给了我。我必须为了「师傅的梦想」将星槎完成。如果无法完成这艘船，那些最珍贵的时光就这样彻底浪费了，不复存在了。
t_en: Yes, Master Ryan has given me his precious time. I must complete the starskiff to fulfill his dream. Otherwise, the time will be wasted and cease to exist.

t_ch: 我，必须完成它，这样才能让一切有意义。
t_en: I have to complete it, to make everything worthwhile.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，谢谢你，又让你奔波了一趟。我会把星槎完成的，等我消息。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, thanks for coming here. I'll tell you when I complete the starskiff.

t_ch: 多亏了你来这里，我更加有动力了。请你之后也要来监督我，鞭策我！
t_en: Your presence really motivates me. Please drop by regularly to monitor my progress and encourage me!

t_ch: 我…必须做到。
t_en: I... must do it.

t_ch: 这是测试星槎的遥控器，你就当它是一款「糟糕的驾驶模拟器」好了。
t_en: This is the remote control used to test the starskiff. Just treat it as a shoddy driving simulator.

t_ch: …别问我为什么不让你上星槎直接试试，我只能说「珍惜生命」。
t_en: ...Don't ask me why you can't hop straight on the starskiff to test it. I wouldn't recommend it if you want to stay safe.

t_ch: （向前行驶）
t_en: (Move forward)

t_ch: （向后行驶）
t_en: (Move backward)

t_ch: （垂直起降）
t_en: (Take off and land vertically)

t_ch: （钟型机动）
t_en: (Bell maneuver)

t_ch: 这艘星槎一动不动。
t_en: The starskiff isn't moving.

t_ch: （向前行驶）
t_en: (Move forward)

t_ch: （向后行驶）
t_en: (Move backward)

t_ch: （垂直起降）
t_en: (Take off and land vertically)

t_ch: （钟型机动）
t_en: (Bell maneuver)

t_ch: 这艘星槎最喜欢的事情之一，就是向那些自以为能够控制星槎的人说「不」。
t_en: This starskiff seems quite disobedient.

t_ch: 遥控器是不是坏掉了？
t_en: Is this remote control broken?

t_ch: 遥控器倒没有坏…你真是太客气了，你应该问星槎是不是坏掉了。
t_en: There's nothing wrong with the remote control. Thanks for being so considerate of my feelings. In fact, you should ask whether the starskiff is broken.

t_ch: 感觉星槎运行得很稳定。
t_en: Feels like the starskiff is moving with high stability.

t_ch: 一动不动，确实很稳定…你真是太客气了，你应该问星槎是不是坏掉了。
t_en: It's because it isn't moving at all. Thanks for being so considerate of my feelings. In fact, you should ask whether the starskiff is broken.

t_ch: 是不是没有能源了？
t_en: Is it out of energy?

t_ch: 这艘星槎能源充足…你真是太客气了，你应该问星槎是不是坏掉了。
t_en: The starskiff has enough energy... Thanks for being so considerate of my feelings. In fact, you should ask whether the starskiff is broken.

t_ch: 星槎是不是坏掉了？
t_en: Is the starskiff broken?

t_ch: 「坏掉」的前提是「它以前是好的」，但它也许从一开始就不是「好的」？
t_en: The term "broken" implies that it used to be working fine. What if it wasn't fine from the beginning?

t_ch: 你试试那个「停航」的操作。
t_en: Try pressing "Stop."

t_ch: （停航）
t_en: (Stop)

t_ch: 不好，这艘星槎要冲出去了……
t_en: Oh, no. The starskiff is going out of control...

t_ch: （钟型机动）
t_en: (Bell maneuver)

t_ch: 多亏了你的灵机一动，就在你选择让星槎做出「钟型机动」之后，玦轮提供的反重力状态被迅速切断，星槎在出港口滑行了一小段之后，反倒是随着惯性掉了下去……
t_en: That's a brilliant move! After you made the starskiff do a bell maneuver, the anti-gravity function provided by the jade wheel was disrupted. The starskiff glided out of the port and fell off out of inertia...

t_ch: 再见了星槎！
t_en: Goodbye, starskiff!

t_ch: （停航）
t_en: (Stop)

t_ch: 你赶紧对星槎做出「停航」操作，不料「停航」反倒是为玦轮提供了动力，星槎全力冲向出港口，很快就消失在你的视野之中，它自由了！
t_en: You quickly pressed "Stop," but it unexpectedly provided power to the jade wheel. The starskiff charged at its full speed out of the port and vanished out of your sight. It's been set free!

t_ch: 再见了星槎！
t_en: Goodbye, starskiff!

t_ch: 铖杰师傅，你的星槎不追回来…没关系吗？
t_en: Chengjie, aren't you going to get your starskiff back?

t_ch: 没关系，就这样吧，反正都是失败作品。
t_en: Just let it be. After all, it's a failure.

t_ch: 我照着师傅剩下的手稿，补全了他的设计。造出了这么一坨垃圾，不可燃的……
t_en: I finished up the design according to my master's manuscript and produced a pile of incombustible junk...

t_ch: 果然，我其实应该找个厂打工或是支个早点摊糊口的。我其实，没有一丁点儿的天赋。
t_en: I really should have worked at a factory or set up a food stall for a living. I really have zero talent for craftsmanship.

t_ch: 我猜刚才那些都是小问题…
t_en: I guess those were all minor issues...

t_ch: 没错，不过就是一些工造设计上的失误嘛。哪里错误改哪里，很快就能解决了！
t_en: You're right. They are just glitches in the artifex design. Just fix what's wrong, and everything will be fine!

t_ch: …要真是小问题就好了。
t_en: ...If only that were true.

t_ch: 刚才那个星槎确实不怎么样。
t_en: Honestly speaking, I think you can do better than that.

t_ch: …很遗憾，我可是铆足了劲，认真打造完成的。
t_en: ...Unfortunately, I already did my best.

t_ch: 老师设计的这款轻型星槎，转弯半径小、速度快、航程也更远。当然，星槎的设计改动越是冒进，操作所需要的精确度就越高。
t_en: This lightweight starskiff designed by my master has a small turning radius, high speed, and can travel far. Of course, the more advanced a design, the higher the precision required to control it.

t_ch: 过去有很多工匠们做过这样的尝试，大多以失败告终。当时的星槎专家认为「轻型星槎的优势在真正的危险面前得不偿失」。
t_en: Most craftsmen who made similar attempts in the past ended up failing. Starskiff experts at that time opined that the advantages of lightweight starskiffs did not contribute much in the face of real dangers.

t_ch: 于是老师设计了与之匹配的「傻瓜式」辅助系统。这套系统能根据不同复杂的场景响应驾驶员的操作，让每个飞行士都能轻松使用轻型星槎。
t_en: Therefore, my master came up with a set-and-forget support system that complemented the starskiff. This system will respond to the pilot's action differently according to different conditions, making it easy for every pilot to maneuver the starskiff.

t_ch: 但是你们看到了，刚才就是所谓的「傻瓜式操作」。
t_en: What you guys witnessed earlier was how the so-called set-and-forget system worked.

t_ch: 确实，傻瓜，操作…
t_en: Well, I must say it isn't very impressive...

t_ch: 是啊，我承认，我就是那个傻瓜
t_en: Exactly. I think I might as well "forget" about the whole thing.

t_ch: 不如把它当成武器，推荐给云骑？
t_en: Shall we pitch this to the Cloud Knights as a weapon?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我现在真的没心情开玩笑…
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm not in the mood for jokes now...

t_ch: 我不知道自己哪一步做错了，每一步我都是按照设计图上来做的呀……
t_en: I have no idea what I messed up. I'm very sure I've done every step according to the manuscript...

t_ch: 你有问过工造司的其他师傅吗？
t_en: Have you consulted other craftsmen of the Artisanship Commission?

t_ch: 手稿里有还未公开的技术……
t_en: There are undisclosed techniques in the manuscript...

t_ch: …你是担心被他人盗取设计。
t_en: ...You're worried that someone might steal the design.

t_ch: 毕竟这是莱恩老师最后的设计了，我比较谨慎。
t_en: After all, this is the last design of Master Ryan. I'm very cautious about it.

t_ch: 难道你还不信任公输师傅？
t_en: Don't you trust Master Gongshu?

t_ch: 其实，我是想偷偷把这艘船造好，让公输师傅大吃一惊来着。
t_en: Actually, I plan to build it secretly to impress Master Gongshu.

t_ch: 不过失败了一次而已，再试试？
t_en: It's just one failure, why not give it another shot?

t_ch: 实在不好意思，虽然你看到的只有这一次，实际上每一次都失败了……
t_en: As a matter of fact, this is just one of my many failures...

t_ch: 我现在也许更应该担心经费缺口的问题了。
t_en: I should probably be more worried about the funding gap now.

t_ch: 你有想过是你师傅自己的错吗？
t_en: Do you think your master might have made a mistake?

t_ch: 不可能！绝不可能！如果有问题，一定是我自己的错，不会是我老师的错。
t_en: No way! That's impossible! If there's a problem, it must be my fault.

t_ch: 老师就不会犯错了吗？太卜大人她就…她好像…确实不犯错。
t_en: Sometimes, masters make mistakes too. Like master diviner, she... she... I think she's never blundered.

t_ch: 对吧？
t_en: Right?

t_ch: 莱恩老师是短生种的天才，而我只是一个不成器的学徒，如果说谁做错了，那毫无疑问就是我做错了嘛。
t_en: While Master Ryan was a genius, I am just an incompetent apprentice. It's easy to see who's more likely to mess up.

t_ch: 再说了，只是想让其他师傅帮我看看的话，也做不到了。就在刚才，星槎也没了。
t_en: Even if I wanted to ask for the opinions of other craftsmen, it is no longer possible now that the starskiff has been ruined.

t_ch: 这样吧！把这「傻瓜式」系统先装在金人身上，然后带到公输师傅那里，让他帮你看看这套…「傻瓜飞行士系统」，如何？
t_en: I have an idea! Let's install the set-and-forget system on an aurumaton and bring it to Master Gongshu so that he can take a look. How does that sound?

t_ch: 这样的话，你既可以询问他技术问题，也可不必公开老师的核心技术。
t_en: In that way, you can consult him without disclosing your master's core techniques.

t_ch: 这不是胡闹吗？
t_en: Doesn't that sound ridiculous?

t_ch: 我看可以。
t_en: I think it can work.

t_ch: …值得一试。这是个很好的方法！
t_en: ...I think it's worth a try!

t_ch: 我现在去借一台金人，为了将这套系统连接进金人，我需要一些能够外接玉兆的零件，这件事情就拜托你们了。
t_en: I'll go and borrow an aurumaton now. To link the system to the aurumaton, I need some parts that can be connected to the jade abacus. I'm counting on you guys for this.

t_ch: 好！找零件的事情就交给{NICKNAME}，我去找公输师傅，咱们分工明确，出发！
t_en: Sure! {NICKNAME} will be in charge of getting the parts, and I'll visit Master Gongshu. Let's go!

t_ch: 你怎么也来了……
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 怎么，你还不想见到我？
t_en: Why? Am I an eyesore to you?

t_ch: 我不是这个意思…抱歉，我撒谎了，我确实是想躲着你们二位的。
t_en: That's not what I mean... I'm sorry for lying. In fact, I was trying to avoid you two.

t_ch: 你们看着我燃起了斗志打算好好奋斗一番，到头来，我还是这样垂头丧气。
t_en: I was all motivated to do my best last time, but in the end, I still lost my fighting spirit.

t_ch: 我来看你设计顺利不顺利。
t_en: I'm here to see how you are faring.

t_ch: 不太顺利，实在没好意思展示给你们看。
t_en: It isn't going well, so I'm too embarrassed to show the design to you.

t_ch: 我看你这艘星槎有模有样的，这不是挺顺利的吗？
t_en: I think it looks good to me. What's wrong?

t_ch: 自从我按照师傅的设计完成了这艘星槎之后，这星槎就一直出故障，根本就没有达到设计要求…你们要是感兴趣的话，我给你们演示一下？
t_en: Since I built the starskiff according to my master's design, the starskiff has malfunctioned a lot of times, and its quality isn't up to par... I'll give you a demonstration if you guys are interested.

t_ch: 我来试试？
t_en: Let me try.

t_ch: 你试试就明白了。
t_en: You'll see what I mean after trying it out.

t_ch: 还能差到哪里去？
t_en: How bad can it be?

t_ch: 毫无正常体验。
t_en: It's not a normal experience whatsoever.

t_ch: 这是一枚可使用在金人身上的标准零件。它们能够衔接外置玉兆与金人的主控系统，可客制化金人行动模式，便于工匠们自由测试。
t_en: This is a standard part that can be used on an aurumaton. It can link the master control system of an aurumaton to an external jade abacus, thus enabling the customization of the aurumaton's action model for craftsmen to perform testing.

t_ch: 说老实话，我也不知道能不能强行把「傻瓜式」驾驶系统做成「傻瓜飞行士系统」…总之我们先试试。你找到了金人零件了吗？
t_en: Frankly speaking, I'm not sure whether I can turn the set-and-forget driving system into a set-and-forget pilot system... Anyway, let's give it a go. Have you found the aurumaton part?

t_ch: 真是太感谢你了，现在需要将这块装着「傻瓜式」系统的玉兆装在金人身上，能麻烦你再帮个忙吗？
t_en: Thanks a lot! Now, we need to fix this jade abacus with the set-and-forget system on the aurumaton. Could you please do me another favor?

t_ch: 这个金人怎么一直在抖动啊？
t_en: Why does this aurumaton keep shaking?

t_ch: 工造司里的老师傅们说，只要让金人一直抖动，看上去就会栩栩如生。
t_en: The veteran craftsmen of the Artisanship Commission always say that as long as you make the aurumaton shake constantly, it will look more lifelike.

t_ch: 我是觉得他们说的也没有什么道理。
t_en: It doesn't make any sense to me.

t_ch: 这玩意儿能坐进星槎驾驶舱？
t_en: Can it fit in the cockpit of the starskiff?

t_ch: …应该坐不进去吧，这不就是个临时的说法吗？
t_en: ...I don't think so.

t_ch: 你是打算让我修理玉兆设备吧。
t_en: You need my help to fix the jade abacus, don't you?

t_ch: 没错，拜托你了！
t_en: Bingo. Please take care of the jade abacus!

t_ch: 万一，我是说万一，公输师傅看出来这套系统的设计逻辑，他会不会质疑……
t_en: If Master Gongshu can figure out the concept behind the system, will he be suspicious...

t_ch: …算了。现在担忧来担忧去也无济于事。
t_en: ...Forget it. Being a worrywart now isn't going to help.

t_ch: 过了不久，青雀就带着公输师傅来到了这里。
t_en: Soon after, Qingque brings Master Gongshu here.

t_ch: 啊…公输师傅，今天有个事情想麻烦你。
t_en: Master Gongshu, there's something I need to trouble you with today.

t_ch: 我已经听这位太卜司小姑娘说过啦，她说你在打造「傻瓜飞行士系统」，想让老夫看看系统毛病出在哪里。
t_en: This girl from the Divination Commission has told me about the set-and-forget pilot system you are working on. She said you needed my help to check what went wrong.

t_ch: 自从莱恩那小子走了之后，我也是很久没看到你小子钻研新技术了。但是这么久了，你小子根本就没来问过我，只顾闭门造车，实在是孺～子～不～可～教～
t_en: Since Ryan passed away, I haven't seen you working on something new for ages. I'm so upset that you've never approached me but chose to do it alone before this!

t_ch: 现在你终于肯来问我了，我倒要看看这是什～么～东～西～
t_en: Now, you're finally seeking my help. I can't wait to look at your masterpiece! Drumroll, please!

t_ch: 公输师傅戏好足啊！
t_en: Master Gongshu is so dramatic!

t_ch: 公输师傅刚才悄悄跟我说，他担忧铖杰师傅好久了，但是碍于面子……
t_en: Master Gongshu just whispered to me that he had been worrying about Chengjie for a long time, but his pride got in the way of approaching Chengjie...

t_ch: 嗯哼哼哼……
t_en: Ahem...

t_ch: 好了好了，我什么都没说。
t_en: Fine, I'll keep my mouth shut now.

t_ch: 拜托你了，公输师傅！
t_en: Please help us, Master Gongshu!

t_ch: 老夫就帮你看看，免得你们费了些没必要的功夫。
t_en: I'll check it to spare you guys from taking unnecessary steps.

t_ch: 铖杰师傅，看看你都错过了什么。
t_en: See what you've missed, Chengjie.

t_ch: 我以为公输师傅，根本看不上我……
t_en: I thought you would look down on me, Master Gongshu...

t_ch: 工造司的学徒们都知道你的技术不行，我也确实看不上你的手艺。
t_en: Your incompetence is known to all apprentices at the Artisanship Commission, and I indeed consider you an unskillful craftsman.

t_ch: 但学徒都是这样的，你要是不来问不来学，一辈子都不会被人看得上。
t_en: But all apprentices have to go through the same thing. If you refuse to ask questions and learn, you won't ever be respected.

t_ch: 莱恩老师也这样说过，公…公输师傅，拜托你了！
t_en: Master Ryan told me the same thing before. M—Master Gongshu, I really need your help!

t_ch: 「傻瓜飞行士系统」…驾驶辅助型金人…有这套系统装在星槎上不就好了吗？嗨，真是搞不懂现在的年轻人……
t_en: You have a set-and-forget pilot system... and a driving support aurumaton... Why don't you fix the system onto the starskiffs? How youngsters nowadays think is beyond me...

t_ch: 公输师傅仔细检查了「金人飞行士」的逻辑设计，并且完成金人行为能力测试。他眉头一皱，发现事情并不简单。
t_en: Master Gongshu examines the design of the aurumaton pilot and completes a test on the aurumaton's abilities. Furrowing his eyebrows, he notices something is amiss.

t_ch: 老夫要说的第一件事儿，就是这套系统跟金人完全不匹配，这是星槎上的系统对吧？
t_en: The first thing I'd like to point out is the incompatibility of the system with the aurumaton. The system belongs to the starskiff, doesn't it?

t_ch: （真不愧是经验老到的师傅……）
t_en: (You really can't hide anything from an experienced craftsman...)

t_ch: …你这样胡乱桥接在金人上，非常危险。
t_en: ...It's very dangerous to modify the aurumaton sloppily like this.

t_ch: 老夫要说的第二件事儿，就是这套系统就算用在星槎上，也完全不行，漏洞百出！
t_en: Secondly, the system won't work even if it's used on a starskiff. It's riddled with errors!

t_ch: 公输师傅何出此言？
t_en: Why do you think so, Master Gongshu?

t_ch: 对呀，我俩对工造的事情一窍不通，公输师傅你这么说肯定是有原因的吧？
t_en: Both of us know next to nothing about artifex or artificium, but I believe you must have a valid reason behind your opinion, Master Gongshu.

t_ch: 很简单，我们做个测试。小娃儿，你先把武器拿好。
t_en: I can show you by performing a test. Please hold the weapon first.

t_ch: 所以才需要你的帮助嘛…
t_en: That's why we need your help...

t_ch: 我说它「漏洞百出」，只是一种客气的说法。
t_en: To say the system is riddled with errors is an understatement.

t_ch: 这要是老夫自己的徒弟做出来的，我就直接说「滚回去重新做」了。
t_en: If this were my apprentice's work, I would have asked him to get out of my sight and redo the system.

t_ch: 真的有这么惨吗？
t_en: Is it really that bad?

t_ch: 很简单，我们做个测试。小娃儿，你先把武器准备好。
t_en: I can show you by performing a test. Please get the weapon ready first.

t_ch: 我们随便输入几个星槎港口的星槎流动数据，按照系统设计，它会根据往来星槎的数量、距离做出及时的判断…一艘、两艘…太卜司小姑娘，你来往上加……
t_en: Now, let's enter some random starskiff traffic flow data of several starskiff ports, and the system will make a timely judgment based on the number and distance of other nearby starskiffs... One, two... Divination Commission girl, please increase the number...

t_ch: 三艘、四艘……
t_en: Three, four...

t_ch: 啊，系统锁死了。
t_en: Argh, the system is locked.

t_ch: 安全模式启动，立刻清除航线威胁！
t_en: Security mode is activated. Threats on the route are to be eliminated immediately!

t_ch: 小娃儿，快拿出你那个铁棒，保护我们！
t_en: Use that iron rod to protect us, kid!

t_ch: 金人又停下来了。
t_en: The aurumaton has stopped moving.

t_ch: 才监控了四艘星槎就已经崩溃了，你想想平时一条航道里的星槎有多少？
t_en: The system collapses while it's monitoring the 4th starskiff. Think about how many starskiffs there are in a lane on a normal day.

t_ch: 上下左右前后，得有几十艘星槎……
t_en: There will be dozens of them from all directions...

t_ch: 这是莱恩那小子做的系统吧？
t_en: Ryan made this system, didn't he?

t_ch: 老师的手稿缺失了一部分，问题全是我擅自补上去的。莱恩老师的原稿肯定没有问题！
t_en: It must be my fault as I am the one who has completed the missing part of my master's manuscript. I'm sure there's no problem with the original version.

t_ch: 就算老夫当着莱恩那小子的面，我也会告诉他说，不行，这个设计完全不行。你知道老夫从来对事不对人，希望你别往心里去。
t_en: If Ryan were here, I would have told him forthrightly that his design wouldn't work. I'm sure you know I have nothing against you. Please don't take this personally.

t_ch: …莱恩老师不会错的。
t_en: ...Master Ryan couldn't have been wrong.

t_ch: 我看，我留在这里也只是尴尬，老夫就先去忙了。你要是有什么不懂的，就多来问我。
t_en: Well, I think I should get going. Feel free to come to me if you have any questions.

t_ch: 即便是公输师傅那样的工正，也没法拍着胸脯保证自己万无一失…你是不是把莱恩师傅想象得太厉害了，说不定老师的手稿也有纰漏？
t_en: Even someone like Master Gongshu cannot be <unbreak>100%</unbreak> sure that he will never make mistakes... You might be idolizing your master too much. Perhaps, there's a mistake in his manuscript.

t_ch: 莱恩老师也犯过错吧？
t_en: Master Ryan made mistakes before, right?

t_ch: 以前是有过的，但是，这既然是老师最后一份设计稿了……
t_en: He did, but this is, after all, his last manuscript...

t_ch: 那还是有可能出错的嘛。
t_en: That doesn't mean he wouldn't make mistakes.

t_ch: 青雀你太刁难铖杰师傅了。
t_en: Qingque, you're being too harsh on Chengjie.

t_ch: 我的工作雷达很准的，它现在就在警告我「别继续了，全是无用功」……
t_en: My work radar is pretty accurate. It's trying to get me to stop because my effort will be in vain...

t_ch: 铖杰师傅你不是想造出一艘星槎，只是想完成它寻求解脱，这很危险。
t_en: Chengjie, building a starskiff is not your true intention, given that you only want to finish it for a sense of closure. This is a dangerous mindset.

t_ch: 你们还记得吗？这份手稿是「师傅的梦想」，我必须要完成它。
t_en: Do you still remember the manuscript is my master's dream? I have to complete it by hook or crook.

t_ch: 那就修补漏洞继续制作吧。
t_en: Well, then you should fix the errors and continue working on the design.

t_ch: …我不行的，只有莱恩老师本人才能完成这份设计稿，我不行的。
t_en: ...I can't. Only Master Ryan himself can complete his manuscript, not me.

t_ch: 要是莱恩老师还在就好了，这样他就能告诉我他是怎么想的了。
t_en: How I wish Master Ryan were still around. He would have been able to explain his design to me.

t_ch: 不行，你这样是完成不了的。
t_en: If you think that way, you won't succeed.

t_ch: 我的水平完全不够，不过是在狗尾续貂而已，只有老师他才能补上缺失的部分。
t_en: My skill isn't up to snuff, and I'm a poor successor to my master. Only my master can complete the missing part.

t_ch: 要是莱恩老师还在就好了，这样他就能告诉我他是怎么想的了。
t_en: How I wish Master Ryan were still around. He would have been able to explain his design to me.

t_ch: 要是复活莱恩老师就好了。
t_en: If only Master Ryan could be brought back to life.

t_ch: 对啊，要是莱恩老师还在，他就能告诉我他是怎么想的了。
t_en: Indeed. How I wish Master Ryan were still around. He would have been able to explain his design to me.

t_ch: 我说，如果莱恩师傅能亲口告诉你呢？
t_en: What if Master Ryan could explain to you face-to-face?

t_ch: 莱恩老师不是死了吗？
t_en: Isn't Master Ryan already dead?

t_ch: 是去世了，但在仙舟上去世，是另一回事。
t_en: He is, but death in Xianzhou is different.

t_ch: 难不成你还会召魂吗？
t_en: Can you summon his soul?

t_ch: 我自己当然不行啦！但，找对人的话，说不定可以喔。
t_en: Of course, I can't! But it might be possible if you get the right person to help you.

t_ch: 我在太卜司的书库里翻阅典籍时看到过，在仙舟上，生命并没有严格意义的终结……
t_en: According to a book I flipped through at the repository of Divination Commission, life on Xianzhou doesn't end in a strict sense...

t_ch: 十王司判官会在一个生命抵达自己的极限之前——比如仙舟人堕入魔阴身之前会被带走，以灵魂的形式送入因果殿。
t_en: When someone's life is on the brink of death — for example, when they become mara-struck — a judge of the Ten-Lords Commission will take them away and send their soul to the Hall of Karma.

t_ch: 解释解释，什么叫灵魂的形式？
t_en: What do you mean by their soul?

t_ch: 我忘了你是个异邦来的化外民。那咱们换个说法，把人的意识「拓印」下来，这样好懂些了吧？
t_en: The fact that you're an Outworlder has slipped my mind just now. Let me put it this way — the judge will make a copy of the person's consciousness. Got it now?

t_ch: 十王司？
t_en: Ten-Lords Commission?

t_ch: 是啊，除了六大司部之外，仙舟还有个专司控制长生种寿数、对抗寿瘟祸患的机关「十王司」。它麾下的判官会负责带走和审判仙舟上的所有灵魂。
t_en: Other than the six major commissions, there is a commission named "Ten-Lords Commission" on Xianzhou that is in charge of managing the lifespan of the long-life species and fighting against plagues and disasters. Judges of the commission are responsible for bringing the souls on Xianzhou to trials.

t_ch: 因果殿？
t_en: Hall of Karma?

t_ch: 简单来说，那是位于仙舟底部，无人知其所在的地方。判官们会在那里收留寿尽者的灵魂。
t_en: To put it simply, it's a place at the bottom of Xianzhou, the precise location of which is known to no one. The judges will keep the souls of the dead there.

t_ch: 按你所说，莱恩师傅是孤身一人在仙舟过世的，并无亲友来料理后事。那么他的灵魂、也就是他的意识数据，应该会被收入因果殿里。
t_en: According to what you said, Master Ryan passed away alone on Xianzhou without any relatives or friends taking care of his funeral. In that case, his soul — which is the data of his consciousness — should be stored in the Hall of Karma.

t_ch: 那也就是说，只要找出师傅的意识，就可以向他提问？
t_en: Does that mean I will be able to ask him questions if I manage to locate his consciousness?

t_ch: 不可思议，但可以试试…
t_en: It sounds incredulous, but it's worth trying...

t_ch: 过问死生之事，十王司能答应吗？
t_en: Will the Ten-Lords Commission allow us to meddle in matters of life and death?

t_ch: 惊动逝者，这不妥吧？
t_en: Wouldn't it be inappropriate for us to disturb the deceased?

t_ch: 是喔。过问死生之事，十王司能答应吗？
t_en: Right. Will the Ten-Lords Commission allow us to meddle in matters of life and death?

t_ch: 青雀这么说，想必是能办到？
t_en: The confidence in your tone tells me it's possible.

t_ch: 过问死生之事，十王司能答应吗？
t_en: Will the Ten-Lords Commission allow us to meddle in matters of life and death?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，青雀小姐，我想最后试一试，我想见莱恩师傅一面。
t_en: {NICKNAME} and Qingque, I want to give the design one last try. I wish to meet my master.

t_ch: 不仅是为了请教他问题，我…我想见他。我想让老师告诉我，我接下来该怎么做……
t_en: Not only do I want to ask for his advice, but I... I'd like to see him in person. I hope he can tell me what I should do next...

t_ch: ……{NICKNAME}？你的意思是？
t_en: ...{NICKNAME}? What do you think?

t_ch: 我可是无名客…
t_en: I'm the Nameless...

t_ch: …越难的事越想尝试。
t_en: ...The harder the challenge is, the more I wanna try it.

t_ch: …希望渺茫，但我不想后悔。
t_en: ...The chance is slim, but I want to have no regrets.

t_ch: 好吧，如果非要试试，就得赶时间趁现在了。我听说今天十王司的判官要与太卜大人会晤。
t_en: Well, if you really want to try, you have to hurry. I heard the judge of the Ten-Lords Commission would have a meeting with the master diviner today.

t_ch: 你又是怎么知道的？
t_en: How did you find out about that?

t_ch: 自打建木重生后…太卜一直受命演算未来。这不，她让我这个司库整理呈报占算结果，又在文件上盖了「呈禀十王」的印戳。
t_en: Since the rebirth of Ambrosial Arbor, the master diviner has been ordered to perform divination. So, she asked me, a custodian, to compile and submit the divination results and stamp the reports with the "To the Ten Lords" seal.

t_ch: 时机怎么这么巧？
t_en: How opportune for us.

t_ch: 不然你以为我说这事儿干嘛？
t_en: Why else do you think I'm telling you this?

t_ch: 那么，咱们出发吧？
t_en: Alright. Now, should we hit the road?

t_ch: 好啊，我得先说好，这么麻烦的事儿，两位得自己努力争取争取，我能帮的忙都已帮上啦。
t_en: Sure. By the way, you two must rely on yourselves to do all the heavy lifting. I have done everything I can to help.

t_ch: 十王司若要太卜司对这些叛徒有更精确的占算，就要提供充足的信息。
t_en: If the Ten-Lords Commission wants a more precise divination from the Divination Commission, they will need to provide enough information.

t_ch: 吾给的已够多了。不仅将卷宗向汝等开放，还亲手拘提罪囚……
t_en: I've provided more than enough. Not only have I made the dossier available to you, but I also captured the criminals myself...

t_ch: 「药王秘传」的人落到你手里，都死的透透的。你要本座对着命途已绝的死人算些什么？
t_en: Those from the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus have all died in your hands. What sort of divination can I do for dead people?

t_ch: 哼。孽物凶险，留手不杀反受其害。死人也是能提供证据的。
t_en: Hmph, if I didn't kill them, I would have been harmed by them. I can collect evidence from them even though they are dead.

t_ch: 我听说判官有「拘、锁、刑、问」四部职责，你该不会是专干处刑这一块的吧？
t_en: As far as I know, a judge has four duties: Detention, interrogation, incarceration, and punishment. You aren't in charge of the punishment, are you?

t_ch: 那为了完成任务，就请判官睁一只眼闭一只眼，将手下留情的职责交给我的人马吧。
t_en: I'd appreciate it if you could turn a blind eye and leave the merciful duty to my people here for them to complete their mission.

t_ch: 你的人马？
t_en: Your people?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，青雀，还有那个工造司的小子，上前来吧！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Qingque, and the young man from the Artisanship Commission, please come over here!

t_ch: 本座在此候你们多时了。
t_en: I've been waiting for you here for a long time.

t_ch: 又被你算到了？
t_en: Did you foresee our visit?

t_ch: 哎？我只是随随便便翻了翻文件，没想到也在太卜的预判之内吗？
t_en: Eh? Did you also predict that I would flip through the documents?

t_ch: 你算错了！我只是来闲逛的。
t_en: Your divination was wrong! I'm just here to check out the place.

t_ch: 哦？那太卜司可不欢迎闲人。
t_en: Oh? We don't welcome visitors here.

t_ch: 唉，看来咱们今天的一举一动，都被她算准了。
t_en: Alas, it seems like she's predicted everything we've done so far.

t_ch: 糟糕，中了跑腿陷阱了…
t_en: Oh, no. We've been tricked into running errands...

t_ch: 你是将军的贵客，是与咱们仙舟结缔盟谊的好帮手…咳，好朋友。把判官大人的活儿托付给你，本座很放心。
t_en: You're an honored guest of the general and a great helper — ahem, friend — of Xianzhou. I have no problem entrusting you with the work of the judge.

t_ch: 先把你们的请求，与来龙去脉讲清楚吧。
t_en: Now, please tell me what you need and the ins and outs of the matter.

t_ch: 铖杰将事情的始末讲了出来，并期望能够获得帮助。
t_en: Chengjie explains the matter, hoping to get help.

t_ch: 我想要和已经去世的莱恩师傅说上话，请太卜大人、判官大人帮帮我。
t_en: I'd like to meet my master, Master Ryan, who has passed away. Please help me.

t_ch: 按十王司律条，魂魄进入因果殿便不得重返阳世。
t_en: According to the law of the Ten-Lords Commission, souls that have entered the Hall of Karma can never return to the world of the living.

t_ch: 不过…令有缓急，事分轻重。身为十王司的判官，吾有便宜行事的职权，若你们能替太卜带去她所要的活口…吾也不是不能行个方便？
t_en: However... Things should be done in order of priority, and I can make decisions at my discretion as the judge of the Ten-Lords Commission. If you can bring back the living people the master diviner is looking for, I don't mind helping you guys.

t_ch: 眼前的宙合阵，「察宙合之势」，这是专用来调取「时间」的阵基。
t_en: This Temporal Terminal that observes the potential of the temporal plane is a base terminal designed to retrieve information related to time.

t_ch: 「莱恩•玛斯特隆佐，大约是一百二十年前进入了因果殿」…我这边将信息输入进去了，阵枢那边就交给你了。
t_en: "Ryan Masteronzo was sent to the Hall of Karma about <unbreak>120</unbreak> years ago." I've entered the info on my side, and I'll leave you to do your part at the base terminals.

t_ch: 眼前的界寰阵，「观寰宇之变」，这是专用来调取「空间」的阵基。
t_en: This Spatial Terminal that observes the changes in the universe is a base terminal designed to retrieve information related to space.

t_ch: 「莱恩•玛斯特隆佐，逝于工造司格物院」…我这边将信息输入进去了，阵枢那边就交给你了。
t_en: "Ryan Masteronzo passed away at the Sapientia Academe of the Artisanship Commission." I've entered the info on my side, and I'll leave you to do your part at the base terminals.

t_ch: 眼前的业成阵，「识业因之序」，这是专用梳理「因果」联系的阵基。
t_en: This Karma Terminal that recognizes the order of karma is a base terminal designed to sort out causal relationships.

t_ch: 「莱恩•玛斯特隆佐，曾为工造司匠人，因年老心力衰竭而逝」…我这边将信息输入进去了，阵枢的事情就交给你了。
t_en: "Ryan Masteronzo, once an Artisanship Commission craftsman, died of old age and heart failure." I've entered the info on my side, and I'll leave you to do your part at the base terminals.

t_ch: 没想到穷观阵还能这样反过来使用，以确切的时间、空间、因果追溯定位人的信息。
t_en: I never thought that the Matrix of Prescience could be used to locate people retroactively using precise information of time, space, and karma.

t_ch: 将特定「某个人的信息」组合起来，便能在在因果殿中找到莱恩师傅的「灵魂」。
t_en: After combining all the information, we'll be able to find Master Ryan's soul in the Hall of Karma.

t_ch: 走吧，接下来就该请莱恩师傅「还阳」了。
t_en: Let's go and invite Master Ryan back to the world of the living.

t_ch: 接下来，只等雪衣判官将逝者接引出来了。
t_en: Next, we'll wait for judge Xueyi to summon the deceased out.

t_ch: 将凭依之物准备妥当。
t_en: Please prepare the body needed for Master Ryan to inhabit.

t_ch: 好、好的…我把金人带过来了。
t_en: S—Sure... I've brought the aurumaton over here.

t_ch: 因果殿里真的是人的灵魂吗？
t_en: Are they really human souls in the Hall of Karma?

t_ch: 若将「持续存在的我」视为统一之物，那么因果殿里存放的便是「灵魂」了。
t_en: If you perceive your continued existence as the same one, then what is inside the Hall of Karma is the souls.

t_ch: 但对异邦人而言，这东西只是信息的拓片罢了，是假合之身留在世间的印记。
t_en: But for travelers, this is just something that carries information and a mark left behind by an artificial body.

t_ch: 为什么雪衣判官可以做到这些事？
t_en: Why do you have the power to do these things?

t_ch: 身为判官，吾身与因果殿相连。勾摄魂魄，裁断业报，若连这些都做不到，算什么判官呢？
t_en: As a judge, my body is connected to the Hall of Karma. I wouldn't be a proper judge if I was unable to collect souls and decide on karmic consequences.

t_ch: 开始接引吧。
t_en: Let's get started.

t_ch: 离魂阴灵，长眠入冥，长梦觉迷，托体赋形！
t_en: The wandering soul in a deep slumber, please wake up and possess the body!

t_ch: 十王判官，在此敕令——莱恩•玛斯特隆佐，速速苏醒！
t_en: By the order of the Ten Lords, wake up now, Ryan Masteronzo!

t_ch: 铖杰师傅带来的金人偃偶缓缓站起身。
t_en: Chengjie's aurumaton puppet slowly stands up.

t_ch: *沉闷的机械低吼声*
t_en: *A muffled mechanical roar*

t_ch: 莱、莱恩老师？
t_en: M—Master Ryan?

t_ch: 我做了好长的一个梦…我记得我还在工造司的办公桌前……
t_en: I had a very long dream... Before that, I was sitting at my desk at the Artisanship Commission...

t_ch: 铖杰？我这是…在俯视你？我有这么高吗……
t_en: Chengjie? Am I looking down at you from a distance? Where am I...

t_ch: 莱恩师傅，您还记得吗？老师，他如今出师困难，需要您的指导。所以，我们将您从长眠中请了出来，请不要见怪。
t_en: Master Ryan, Chengjie is having difficulty mastering craftsmanship and needs your guidance. So, we've disturbed your eternal rest. Please don't hold it against us.

t_ch: 原来我已死了……
t_en: Oh, I'm dead...

t_ch: 巨大的金人缓缓移动着笨重的身躯，四下环顾。最后，终于仿佛从那场漫长、昏暗的冥梦中醒了过来。
t_en: The enormous aurumaton moves its bulky body slowly and scans its surroundings. Then, it moved as if waking up from a long and bleak dream.

t_ch: 铖杰，没想到我还能回来，给你再上一课。
t_en: Chengjie, I never thought I could come back and teach you again.

t_ch: 逝者冥寐未尽，暂得清醒。尔等须抓紧时间，切莫闲聊，徒留遗憾。
t_en: The deceased's lucidity only lasts for a brief moment. Please hurry up and skip the chitchatting.
